narrative:
Ali Ahmed is a Left Midfield player representing Canada at the FIFA World Cup 2026. His overall rating of 7.25/10 places him in the top 3% of left midfields at this tournament. This rating is significantly influenced by his club form over the last two seasons, accounting for 63% of the total.

At this World Cup, Ahmed demonstrates strong defensive work rate with 2.669 tackles and 1.144 interceptions per 90 minutes. However, his attacking output is currently lower than his club level, registering 0.087 expected goals and 0.122 expected assists per 90 minutes. His 0.763 shots per 90 minutes also indicate a more conservative offensive presence in the tournament so far.

Ahmed appears to be a crucial defensive presence for Canada, providing stability on the left flank across his 236 minutes played. Scouts and fans should observe if he can elevate his offensive contributions, aiming to reach his club level of 0.218 xG and 0.173 xA per 90 minutes. His ability to combine defensive solidity with increased attacking threat will be key in the remaining matches.
